description: Benchmark target-context taxonomy + claim-scope schema (Bastion B17, closes RR-BAS-03). Every canonical benchmark artifact must declare its target_context so a microbenchmark cannot be mistaken for Bitcoin Core / libbitcoin node throughput, and a GPU public-data / CPU-fallback benchmark cannot be presented as a general native-engine speed claim. Enforced by ci/check_bench_target_context.py (folded into ci/check_bench_doc_consistency.py and co-gated by ci/perf_security_cogate.py).
field_location: For docs/bench_unified_*.json the context fields live under the top-level `metadata` object; for docs/BITCOIN_CORE_BENCH_RESULTS.json they live at the top level. The gate reads `target_context` from either location.

rules.context_required: target_context must be present and one of target_context_enum.
rules.claim_scope_required: a benchmark artifact carrying timings must declare claim_scope (a performance number without a declared scope is a scope_mismatch).
rules.gpu_not_native: target_context in (gpu_public_data) must NOT carry native_hardware_claim=true; a gpu_public_data / fallback benchmark is never native GPU-hardware performance evidence.
rules.integration_evidence_required: target_context in (bitcoin_core, libbitcoin) must reference integration evidence (an integration_evidence field naming docs/BITCOIN_CORE_TEST_RESULTS.json / docs/INTEGRATION_EVIDENCE_TABLE.md or equivalent).
rules.owner_gated_explicit: target_context = unknown_owner_gated must carry an explicit owner_gated note (notes mentioning 'owner_gated').
rules.security_gate_dependency_required: every artifact must name a security_gate_dependency (CT / determinism / integration gate that must be green for the perf claim to be valid).
rules.corrupt_timing_blocking: zero / negative / non-finite / impossible timings remain blocking (enforced by ci/check_bench_doc_consistency.py check_bench_artifact_sanity, Bastion B8).
